title: Bayo Alaba - For Southend East and Rochford
meta_description: I'm Bayo Alaba, and I aim to become Southend East and Rochford's first Labour MP.
headings: h2: It can be hard to find the right help
h2: What I can help with
h2: What I can’t help with
h3: "Stay Informed, Stay Engaged – Follow Us for Updates and the Latest News!"
h3: Stay Informed Southend and Rochford Matters
text_excerpt: Bayo Alaba - For Southend East and Rochford Skip to content → Bayo Alaba Home Pride in Shoeburyness Chair Applications Board Member Applications Have Your Say News Get Help Stay Informed Home Pride in Shoeburyness Chair Applications Board Member Applications Have Your Say News Get Help Stay Informed Can I help you? It can be hard to find the right help Sometimes when faced with an issue like being served with a section 21 eviction notice or struggling with navigating the complexities of PIP renewal or applying for a parking scheme around your property, it can be an overwhelming time knowing what is out there or who can help. As your MP they are things we can help with or sometimes your local authority or a community organisation is best placed to help. Check if you are a constituent at members.parliament.uk . Community Organisation South Essex Community Hub Website: https://sech-uk.com/ Telephone: 01702 611199 Provides support with housing applications, benefits, navigating local services and more. Located in the Victoria Shopping Centre with both drop-in and appointment services. South Essex Advocacy Services Website: https://www.southessexadvocacy.org/ Telephone: 01702 340566 Supports vulnerable or isolated adults by helping them understand their rights and access appropriate services. Citizens Advice Southend Website: https://www.citizensadvicesouthend.org.uk/ Telephone: 01702 456352 Provides free advice on housing, employment, benefits, debt and energy issues. Rochford Community Hub Email: [email protected] Telephone: 01268 779999 Rochford District Neighbourhood Watch Email: [email protected] Telephone: 01268 770519 South Essex Extended Services Email: [email protected] Telephone: 01702 545771 Citizens Advice Braintree and South Essex Telephone: 0808 278 7877 Homelessness support services St Vincent’s Southend Email: [email protected] Telephone: 01702 592980 One Love Project Email: [email protected] Salvation Army Southend Email: [email protected] Telephone: 01702 348999 HARP Telephone: 01702 615000 Royal British Legion Website: https://www.britishlegion.org.uk/ Email: [email protected] Telephone: 0808 802 8080 Food Banks Storehouse Website: https://www.southendvineyard.org.uk/storehouse/ Southend Foodbank Website: https://southend.foodbank.org.uk/ The Shoebury Ark Website: https://shoeburyark.org.uk/ </p Mental Health Support If you’re struggling with your mental health, support is available. Samaritans Website: https://www.samaritans.org/ Telephone: 116 123 Available 24 hours a day, 365 days a year Support if you’re finding it hard to cope Support if you’re worried about someone else Cost of Living Support Southend Residents Website: https://www.southend.gov.uk/costofliving Rochford Residents Website: https://www.rochford.gov.uk/cost-of-living Community Safety Essex Police Website: https://www.essex.police.uk/ Crimestoppers Website: https://crimestoppers-uk.org/news-media/community/Essex Health Services Find a GP Website: https://www.nhs.uk/service-search/find-a-gp Find a Dentist Website: https://www.nhs.uk/nhs-services/dentists/how-to-find-an-nhs-dentist/ Mid and South Essex NHS Foundation Trust Website: https://www.mse.nhs.uk/ Essex Partnership University NHS Foundation Trust Website: https://www.eput.nhs.uk/ What I can help with Housing enquiries (including banding allocation updates/reviews, disrepair, homelessness) Universal credit issues Health (including treatment delays and referrals to Social Care) Enquiries to Government departments such as the Department for Work and Pensions and HMRC. Education (SEND issues and difficulties with schooling) What I can’t help with I cannot provide legal advice nor immigration advice as this is something I am not permitted to do due parliamentary protocol I also cannot influence Court decisions Local Council Issues Many everyday issues are handled directly by your local council. Reporting them directly can often resolve the issue faster. Southend City Council – Report an Is
